description: Confounder: The model assumes rms_norm_no_affine always forces RMS to 1, but when the input

_meta.explanation: The rule 'When lipschitz=True, AdaLayerNormContinuous applies rms_norm_no_affine to both scale and shift, forcing their RMS to 1 in the input dtype (e.g., bfloat16), regardless of the original magnitude.' does NOT hold here. The model assumes rms_norm_no_affine always forces RMS to 1, but when the input is all zeros (as with emb=zeros and bias=False), normalization produces NaNs or zeros, breaking the RMS=1 expectation due to division by zero (even with eps, the gradient or numerical behavior in bfloat16 can collapse the result)
_meta.curriculum_note: Rule 'When lipschitz=True, AdaLayerNormContinuous applies rms_norm_no_affine to both scale and shift, forcing their RMS to 1 in the input dtype (e.g., bfloat16), regardless of the original magnitude.' holds in the obvious case but breaks here. Distractor B is the hard negative — the rule naively applied.
